changes.description: New Calendar Work-Item Assistant lets you select any work item (and its descendants) on the Calendar, chat with an AI to propose Description and Acceptance Criteria improvements, review a per-item diff, manually edit any proposed change, then apply directly to Azure DevOps with one click
Draggable, resizable floating dialog — grab the header to reposition anywhere on screen; drag any edge or corner to resize; minimise to a title bar when not in use
Current content panel shows the live Description and Acceptance Criteria of all selected items before you start chatting, so you always have context on what you are changing
Inline editing in the diff review lets you manually adjust the AI-proposed text before applying — edits are saved back to the proposal and reflected in the diff
All ADO writes go through a mandatory review-and-confirm gate; the assistant can only propose changes, not write to ADO directly — revisions conflict are caught per item with stale-revision detection

Chat thread now shows a live tool-activity pill (spinning indicator + tool name) while the agent reads files or searches the codebase, and animated dots while waiting for the first response token — no more fragmented pill-per-word rendering
What's New now loads release notes from a unified server API so the modal always reflects the version actually deployed
Unread release badge and What's New banner now appear throughout the app — not only on the project picker
What's New modal opens automatically on login when a new release is available and auto-show is enabled
Fixed release notes and unread state falling out of sync when the deployed app was newer than the database version record
Floating action button is now draggable — reposition it anywhere on screen and your placement is remembered across sessions
Feature Requests admins can drag-and-drop rows to set team priority order when sorted by rank
Feature Requests now appears in project menu visibility settings so admins can control who sees it
Feature request AI analysis now respects the configured skill provider (GitHub or ADO)
GitHub-hosted skills load correctly when starting agent conversations
Admins can view and update feature requests regardless of which project submitted them
Added Ask Apex — a product assistant chat that answers questions about features, workflows, navigation, and the roadmap, accessible from the floating action button on every page
Added Feature Requests — any user can submit product ideas via the floating action button, and Apex admins can triage, rank, and manage requests from the new Feature Requests view
Incoming feature requests are automatically analyzed by AI with suggested priority, risk level, and rationale to help the team prioritize
Added a collapsible sidebar for primary navigation on desktop — replaces the header view switcher and remembers your collapsed/expanded preference
Ask Apex now loads live product documentation and can search the codebase for deeper answers beyond its baseline knowledge
Project admins can configure the feature request analysis skill and AI model in Project Settings
Skills can now be loaded from GitHub repositories as an alternative to the local skill catalog
Platform admins can create and manage feature flags with targeting rules for everyone, specific projects, users, or groups — including enable/disable toggles, lifecycle status, and a full audit log
Client apps can gate features with useFeatureFlag — flags are evaluated per user and project so rollouts can be scoped without redeploying
Platform Admin is now organized into tabs for Access & Users, Menu Visibility, and Feature Flags
My Work and Standup menu items respect per-project menu visibility settings configured in Platform Admin
Fixed login and OAuth failures on scaled Azure App Service instances by persisting sessions to shared storage across workers
Added AI-guided Daily Standup ceremonies — participants receive a personal standup thread, answer yesterday/today/blockers with an agent, and submit updates from the My Standup page
Standup Management lets admins configure scheduled standups per project and group, run sessions on demand, trigger the facilitator early, and review recent session status
A facilitator agent automatically summarizes team updates, creates cross-cutting follow-up discussions, and publishes a session summary when everyone submits or the deadline is reached
Standup Summary view shows completed session summaries and follow-up items for the team
Standup configs support multiple groups, configurable reminder timing, and a facilitator deadline instead of a fixed four-hour window
Standup Management timing controls use modern schedule-time and duration pickers with quick-select presets for reminders and facilitator deadlines
Project skill settings now include a Standup Skill path and model so teams can customize the participant standup procedure
Participants receive in-app and Teams notifications when a standup session starts and periodic reminders until they submit
PRDs, design docs, and design prototypes now require a two-step approval: assigned reviewers approve first, then the document owner gives final approval before the item is marked approved
The PRD review header includes an Approvals panel showing reviewer and owner approval status across PRD, Design Doc, Design Prototype, and QA review groups
Interview kickoff now requires selecting Design Prototype and QA reviewers alongside PRD and Design Doc reviewers, and those selections are used automatically when submitting for review
QA reviewers can approve generated test cases during PRD review without waiting for the full PRD approval cycle
Section owner pickers on interview kickoff are filtered by role group — BA/Product Owner for PRD, Developer for design docs, UI/UX for prototypes, and QA for test cases
The backlog summary bar now shows the total generated test case count alongside features and PBIs
PRD review actions are consolidated into a header overflow menu for a cleaner toolbar
Local dev login offers persona buttons for Developer, BA, Manager, Product Owner, QA, and UI/UX so you can test role-specific flows without SSO
Inline review comment highlights no longer jump to the wrong occurrence when the same text appears multiple times in a section
Resolved review comments no longer re-anchor after document edits
The PRD assistant now refreshes generated test cases automatically after a run that adds or updates test cases
Start New Interview no longer briefly appears enabled before group permissions finish loading
Start New Interview is now restricted to members of the BA, Manager, or Product-Owner groups — users without group membership see the button disabled with a tooltip explaining the requirement
The server now enforces group membership on interview creation, so the restriction is upheld even if the UI is bypassed
User group memberships are now included in the permissions API response and exposed via useAppShell for client-side access control checks
Design docs now generate automatically when each design prototype is approved, grounded in the interview transcript, PRD, and that feature's prototype HTML

Create in ADO now exports Technical Backlog Items separately from PBIs, links dependencies between work items, attaches design doc files and prototype HTML to features, and creates ADO test cases from generated QA suites
The Create in ADO modal shows a results panel after export with created work items, dependency links, and direct links to ADO
Removed the Design Doc Q&A interview phase — design docs start generating immediately after prototype approval instead of waiting for a separate Q&A step
Design doc groups on the interviews dashboard show an approval progress bar with approved/total counts
Backlog features now carry designDocId and designPrototypeId links for reliable matching when gating ADO creation
PRD status now refreshes automatically when test case generation completes so validation transitions appear without a manual reload
Interviews, PRDs, design docs, and design prototypes now record which AI model was used for generation and show it in each review view
The model selected when starting an interview or generating a PRD is persisted through the workflow, including per-feature design docs split from a parent doc
Design prototype generation now stores the resolved Bedrock model (project skill override or default) on each prototype, including retries
Added project access requests on the project selector so users can request access to ADO and non-ADO projects before entering the app
Platform admins can now review project access requests and manage project assignments, including pending email assignments for users who have not logged in yet
What's New updates now appear as a non-blocking banner on the project selector page instead of interrupting users after they choose a project
Release announcement tracking now syncs through a server-side setting so the latest changelog version can be updated without relying only on startup file reads

Added Apex Assistant button to PRD view (when not yet approved) — opens a resizable slide-out chat panel pre-loaded with the full PRD, backlog, and all reviewer comments so the AI can bulk-address feedback and propose content changes
AI-proposed PRD or backlog changes are staged for review — a GitHub-style diff banner appears with Accept and Reject buttons so authors control exactly what gets applied
New resolve_prd_comment MCP tool lets the Apex Assistant automatically mark reviewer comments as resolved after addressing them in the content, closing the feedback loop without manual intervention

Replaced the full-document Edit tab with a header Edit button (opens a focused modal) and per-section pencil icons in the Preview tab — hover a section heading to edit just that section without touching the rest of the document
Backlog tab is now editable — each epic, feature, and item card has an edit icon that opens a pre-filled form, letting authors update titles, descriptions, and priorities without leaving the PRD view
Reviewers can now highlight text in PRDs and Design Docs to leave inline comments — a sidebar panel shows all threads with reply, resolve, reopen, and delete actions
Approve button is blocked until all inline review comments are resolved, ensuring no document is approved with outstanding feedback
Owner and assigned reviewer names are now always visible in the document header, regardless of document status
Replaced the Request Revision modal with the inline comment workflow — reviewers leave structured, text-anchored feedback instead of a single free-text reason
Assign PRD and Design Doc owners when starting an interview — a modal lets you select section owners from active users, and each owner receives an in-app notification on assignment
PRD and Design Doc owner names are now displayed as chips in the interview header
Chat agent retries transient run failures automatically and shows a live 'Retrying…' indicator in the conversation instead of immediately surfacing an error
Error messages in interview chat now include a '↺ Try again' button to re-send the last user message without leaving the chat

Interview chat shows a context usage bar and wrap-up banners as the conversation approaches the model limit — with one-click Generate PRD and a confirmation before sending when the window is nearly full
Chat agents automatically retry transient and rate-limited Cursor API errors with exponential backoff, and classify failures so auth issues prompt a refresh instead of endless retries
Interview chat sessions use a longer idle timeout (2 hours) and the server recovers threads left stuck in running after a restart
Failed sends and PRD generation in interviews now show a dismissible error banner instead of failing silently
Azure Application Insights telemetry for client and server when APPLICATIONINSIGHTS_CONNECTION_STRING is configured
Approvers are now assigned per-document at submit time — a modal lets authors pick specific PRD and design doc approvers from the project pool before submitting for review
In-app notification center — a bell icon in the header shows unread notifications; approvers receive a notification when assigned to review a document
Real-time notification delivery via Server-Sent Events — new notifications appear instantly without page refresh, with optional toast popups
Notification preferences — users can toggle per-type notifications and toast alerts on or off from the Notifications page
Admins can reassign pending approvers on any document after submission via a new assignments panel
Approval mode (any one / all required) is now configurable per project in Admin → Project Settings
Design doc approver IDs set at PRD submission are automatically propagated to new per-feature design docs as they are generated
Admins can assign designated design doc and PRD approvers per project — any listed approver with review permission can approve that document type
Each project can override the global default AI model with its own per-project default
Project settings admin page is reorganized into accordion sections with contextual help, making skill paths, models, and pills easier to configure
Project list view shows approver counts at a glance instead of a wide table of truncated model IDs
Generate PRD button on completed interviews is now disabled when a PRD has already been generated, preventing accidental duplicates
Reopen button on completed interviews is disabled (and hidden from the locked notice) when a PRD already exists, keeping the interview locked once a PRD is in progress
Reopen for Review button on PRDs is disabled when the PRD status is approved, preventing approved PRDs from being sent back for re-review

Fixed issue where selecting a Quick Skill Pill did not apply the model configured for that pill — switching between pills or selecting one without a configured model could silently carry over the wrong model from a previous session

title: AI Fix Validation & Per-Feature Design Docs

changes.description: Users can now trigger an AI-powered fix for validation gaps on design docs — the agent proposes targeted edits and surfaces a side-by-side review panel before any changes are accepted or reverted
Users can cancel an in-progress validation run to immediately return a design doc to draft status
When Q&A produces content for multiple features, the system now automatically creates separate design doc rows per feature instead of collapsing them into one
Design docs from the same PRD are now grouped in the dashboard with an expandable card showing per-status counts and a bulk-delete action
Removed the 'Rejected' status from PRDs and design docs — reviewers now use 'Request Revision' as the only decline action, simplifying the review workflow
Validation report now auto-generates a fallback from the scorecard when no markdown report exists yet, so the Validation Report tab is never blank after scoring completes
Fixed validation report polling firing when the doc was not actively validating, which caused unnecessary background requests
Admins can now configure Quick Skill Pills — clickable shortcut buttons on the Agent home page that route messages through a specific skill and model
Agent home page now requires selecting a skill pill before typing, providing a guided entry point instead of an open-ended prompt
Removed project pill and model selector from the compose view for a cleaner, distraction-free experience
Design docs can now be scored by an AI validation agent before approval — a configurable validation skill produces a structured scorecard and blocks approval until the score reaches 90%
Added a Validation Report tab on design doc pages that displays the agent's scorecard in real time, with a color-coded score badge visible from the header
Admins can now configure a dedicated validation skill and model per project in Admin Project Settings
Added admin-only 'Reopen for Review' action on PRDs to force any PRD back to Pending Review status
Agent output artifacts (PRDs, design docs, validation scorecards) are now synced directly to Postgres after each run, replacing file-based durable storage for better cloud resilience
Chat threads now load from Postgres instead of disk JSON files, so conversations survive restarts and multi-instance deployments
Added startup recovery loop that re-hydrates in-flight PRD, design doc, and validation watchers after a server restart, plus graceful shutdown to drain connections cleanly
Fixed an async call in the ADO MCP server that could silently fail when looking up a chat thread

title: Design Doc Q&A Phase & AI Assistant

changes.description: Design docs now support a pre-generation Q&A interview phase — when a Q&A skill is configured, an AI-guided conversation collects requirements before the document is generated
Added a persistent AI Assistant panel on each Design Doc page for interactive editing, Q&A, and refinement against the live document content
Admin project settings now expose dedicated skill and model selectors for the Design Doc Q&A phase and the Design Doc Assistant
Added Amergis dark theme with teal accents — select it alongside Light and Dark in the user menu theme picker
Upgraded theme selector from a two-state toggle to a dedicated Light / Dark / Amergis picker in the user menu
What's New popup now reliably appears after each deployment, tracked server-side per user instead of relying on browser cache
Added 'Show automatically on login' toggle in the What's New modal so users can opt out of the auto-popup while still seeing the unread indicator
User display name now shown at the top of the user menu for quick account identification
Rebranded the app as Apex with a new primary-blue visual identity, browser tab icon, sign-in experience, project selector, header mark, and chat home branding

Updated light mode to use the Apex primary-blue palette and refreshed dark mode with black and charcoal surfaces plus blue accents across navigation, buttons, calendar events, focus states, and shared UI surfaces
Clicking the Apex mark in the menu bar now returns you to the project selector so you can switch projects more easily
Added design doc generation from approved PRDs, including a dedicated review page with editable design, tech spec, and assumptions sections
Added a Design Docs tab in the interviews dashboard with status filters, search, review actions, and deletion support
Admins can now choose project-specific interview, PRD, and design doc skills, plus per-skill model selections and a global default model
PRD approvals can now automatically start design doc generation and link reviewers directly to the generated design doc
Generated design docs now render Mermaid diagrams in the app with theme-aware styling
Azure DevOps automation now skips retired area paths so stale team configurations do not trigger unnecessary checks
What's New modal now opens automatically when landing on the home page if there is a new release you haven't seen — no need to open the profile menu to discover updates
Dismissing the What's New modal now immediately clears the red indicator on the profile icon
What's New header title color now follows the app theme — green in dark mode, blue in light mode
Added full role-based access control system — admins can create and manage roles, assign permissions per role, and control which features each user can access
Admin Roles page — create, edit, and delete roles; manage which permissions each role grants; assign or remove members directly from the role management UI
Admin Users page — view all users who have logged in, assign or revoke roles per user, and search/filter users by name or email
New users are automatically registered on first login and receive the default 'member' role with standard access permissions
Admin routes are protected by permission checks — users without 'admin:roles' are redirected away from /admin/* and cannot access admin API endpoints
Permission-aware navigation — the Admin menu item in the header is only visible to users with the admin:roles permission

title: Chat Agent, Thread History & Follow-Up Flags

changes.description: Added AI chat agent with skill-based workflows — type '/' in the compose box to browse available skills (e.g. grill-with-docs, to-prd) or send freeform questions for open-ended agent conversations
Chat thread history sidebar — browse, resume, and delete past conversations from a scrollable sidebar with status indicators and relative timestamps
Flag conversations for follow-up — click the flag icon on any chat thread to mark it for later; flagged threads show a persistent indicator so you never lose track of important conversations
Flagged-only filter — toggle the flag filter in the history sidebar header to instantly narrow the list to only your flagged conversations, with a badge showing the total count
Chat threads are now persisted in PostgreSQL — conversations survive server restarts and are available across sessions, replacing the previous in-memory-only storage
Proper per-user thread isolation — each user only sees their own chat history, with ownership checks enforced on every thread operation
File attachments in chat — attach up to 5 files per message to provide additional context to the agent during a conversation

title: Selective PBI Creation in Azure DevOps

changes.description: Added '⊕ ADO' button directly on PBI rows in the backlog tree — BAs can now push individual approved PBIs to Azure DevOps without creating the entire epic hierarchy at once
Added '⊕ Create ADO' button in the PBI details panel action bar, available whenever the PBI and its parent Feature are Approved or Accepted and the item hasn't been pushed to ADO yet
Confirmation modal summarises exactly what will be created (PBI only, or Feature + PBI if the Feature is not yet in ADO) before committing to Azure DevOps
If a parent Feature is Approved but not yet in ADO, it is automatically created and linked to its Epic (when the Epic already exists in ADO) as part of the PBI creation flow

After a PBI is successfully created in ADO, the draft wiki document is updated in-place — the PBI and any newly created Feature receive their ADO IDs, URLs, and Merged status without a full page reload
Enhanced roadmap grid view with dynamic column sizing - work items column now expands from 450px to accommodate longer titles without truncation
Enabled horizontal scrolling for month/quarter columns - allows viewing 12+ months while maintaining readability with consistent 200px column widths
Work item titles now wrap intelligently within cards and labels instead of being truncated with ellipsis
Month/quarter date headers now display on a single line without wrapping (e.g., 'Jan 2026', 'Feb 2027') to eliminate confusion when spanning multiple years
Added sticky work items column that remains visible while scrolling horizontally through timeline
Enhanced scrollbar visibility with purple theme color and larger 12px size for easier navigation
Improved responsive behavior across all screen sizes while maintaining consistent month column widths
Added hierarchical backlog view for unscheduled items, similar to Azure DevOps - work items now display in parent-child relationships with expand/collapse controls
Epic-focused navigation - when 'All Types' is selected, only Epics are shown at root level, allowing users to drill down into Features, PBIs, and Bugs
Multiple state selection - users can now select multiple states simultaneously using checkboxes instead of a single dropdown
Redesigned work item cards with cleaner, more professional appearance - neutral backgrounds with subtle colored left borders based on work item type
Unscheduled sidebar now collapses by default to provide more space for the calendar view, and can be expanded up to 1000px wide (increased from 600px)
Enhanced visual hierarchy with proper indentation (20px per level) for child work items
Work item type color coding: Epic (Purple), Feature (Blue), Bug (Red), PBI (Green), TBI (Amber)
Added tag editing functionality in work item details panel - users can now add and remove tags directly from the details view
Added related items section for Feature work items, displaying child PBIs, Technical Backlog Items, and bugs with full navigation support
Enhanced tag display with remove buttons and inline add input for quick tag management
Extended related items functionality to Features, matching the existing capability for PBIs and TBIs
Renamed 'Analytics' view to 'Planning' for clearer purpose alignment
Standardized all tab headers to be left-aligned with consistent 24px font size and 600 font-weight
Unified tab names: 'Cycle Time', 'Developer Stats', 'QA Metrics', 'Roadmap', 'Releases'
Removed center-alignment and max-width constraints from Developer Stats view for full-width consistency
Applied consistent 24px padding across all planning tab views
Added delete functionality for release epics with confirmation modal
Fixed React DOM nesting warning with whitespace in tbody elements
Added missing handleDeleteReleaseEpic function to ReleaseView component
Implemented DELETE /api/releases/:epicId endpoint for removing release epics

title: Enhanced Work Item Details & Rich Content Display

changes.description: Added comprehensive work item details panel showing type-specific fields (Description, Acceptance Criteria, Repro Steps, Design, Discussions)
Implemented rich text field component with collapsible sections for HTML content from Azure DevOps
Added dynamic discussions/comments fetching from Azure DevOps comments API
Expanded Related Items to show parent, child, and related work items (not just children)
Added navigation history with Back button for browsing through related work items
Added Tags section displaying work item tags as styled badges
Increased default Details Panel width to 45% of screen for better content viewing
Updated Details Panel max width to 70% of screen (from fixed 800px)
Redesigned Back button with modern outline style and smooth hover animations
Work item fields now always render even when empty, showing 'No content provided'
Add indicator to profile icon
Added collapsible Unscheduled Items section in agenda view for better space management
Added resizable Details Panel - drag the left edge to adjust width (300-800px)
Added resizable Unscheduled Items list - drag the right edge to adjust width (200-600px)
Work item tags are now properly fetched and displayed
Improved panel resize experience with visual feedback on hover
Added due date change history tracking with reason logging
Added related items view for PBIs and TBIs
Support for QA Complete Date and Target Date fields
Epic progress visualization with child items tracking
Advanced filtering by iteration, work item type, assignee, and state
Search functionality for unscheduled items
Collapsible unscheduled items sidebar
Improved calendar performance with optimized rendering
Calendar view for visualizing work items by due date
Azure DevOps integration for PBIs, TBIs, Epics, Features, and Bugs
Drag and drop to schedule/unschedule items
Work item details panel with inline editing
Cycle time analytics and developer statistics
